name: Hungary

authority.name: Nemzeti Média- és Hírközlési Hatóság (NMHH) — National Media and Infocommunications Authority, Hungarian eIDAS supervisory body and national Trusted List scheme operator
authority.operator: Accredited QTSPs under eIDAS (Microsec zrt. / e-Szignó, NETLOCK Kft., and the Government CA / Kormányzati Hitelesítés Szolgáltató operated by NISZ Nemzeti Infokommunikációs Szolgáltató Zrt., among others)

governingLaw: Reglamento eIDAS (UE) 910/2014 · 2015. évi CCXXII. törvény az elektronikus ügyintézés és a bizalmi szolgáltatások általános szabályairól (E-ügyintézési tv.)

technology.trustList: ETSI TS 119 612 — the Hungarian Trusted List (HU_TL.xml, NMHH)

relatedLinks.label: NMHH — National Media and Infocommunications Authority
EU LOTL — List of the Lists (European Commission)

notes: Hungary's qualified-signature trust is a federation of accredited QTSPs (Microsec zrt. / e-Szignó, NETLOCK Kft., and the Government CA / Kormányzati Hitelesítés Szolgáltató operated by NISZ Nemzeti Infokommunikációs Szolgáltató Zrt., among others) supervised by the Nemzeti Média- és Hírközlési Hatóság (NMHH), the National Media and Infocommunications Authority acting as Hungary's eIDAS supervisory body, and published in the Hungarian national eIDAS Trusted List (HU_TL.xml, ETSI TS 119 612). The 62 currently-granted CA certificates were promoted wholesale after verifying the Trusted List's XAdES signature through the EU LOTL chain of trust: the LOTL signature was checked against the pinned European Commission signing anchor, and the Hungarian TSL's signature (signer: C=HU, L=Budapest, O=National Media and Infocommunications Authority, Hungary, CN=Hungarian Trusted List Scheme Operator; cert SHA-256 264c843f8ef85a822cf9612c680609a0fce3171a7496ec4ca6b810411274f34d) against the identity the LOTL declares for Hungary, so the list's single seal vouches for every certificate it contains (eIDAS Art. 22). Only services with a currently-granted status and a CA / qualified-certificate service type were selected; withdrawn, deprecated, and non-CA services were dropped. Verification evidence is in VERIFICATION.md. Key algorithms across the set are mixed: 23 CAs use RSA-2048, 16 use EC P-384, 11 use RSA-4096, 11 use EC P-256, and 1 uses RSA-3072. Six certificates in the set have passed their notAfter and are retained as archived-but-granted entries; they surface under the Expired status filter.
